old_claim.note: Electronic Health Records provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: The scoped milestone used two-way television cameras and screens to conduct remote clinical examinations and therapy.

source_shape.source_locator: The history describes televised patient examinations and therapy, then identifies the 1964 Omaha-Norfolk microwave link as the first large-scale test of interactive television as a medical medium.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The Nebraska program used live two-way television for remote examination, diagnosis, psychotherapy, and clinical rounds in 1964.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Television is the defining medium in the directly documented video-consult milestone, whereas electronic records are absent and historically later.

ontology_before: Video telemedicine was modeled as requiring electronic health records.
ontology_after: The first video-consult milestone requires television; electronic records remain a later integration.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed and locked by graph invariant 2026-07-12-launch-source-check-completion: telemedicine_video_consults now depends on television and no longer on electronic_health_records.
would_reject_if: The node is narrowed to contemporary EHR-integrated telehealth portals.
Evidence shows the 1964 Nebraska consultations were not conducted through television.
short_reason: television directly supports the scoped system; electronic_health_records does not.
